# savanna

[![Go Version](https://img.shields.io/github/go-mod/go-version/135yshr/savanna-vet-go)](https://go.dev/)
[![Go Report Card](https://goreportcard.com/badge/github.com/135yshr/savanna-vet-go)](https://goreportcard.com/report/github.com/135yshr/savanna-vet-go)
[![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-yellow.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT)

A Go CLI tool that detects test smells in Go test files.

## Overview

savanna analyzes Go test files using AST parsing and detects common test smells — patterns that indicate potential issues with test quality and maintainability. When smells are detected, a lion ASCII art roars with a [@t_wada](https://github.com/t-wada) meme message.

- **Zero external dependencies** — stdlib only
- **Fast** — leverages `go/ast` and `go/parser` for static analysis
- **Configurable** — enable/disable individual smell detectors

## Supported Test Smells

| Smell Type | Description |
|---|---|
| `EMPTY_TEST` | Test function with no statements |
| `MISSING_ASSERTION` | Test function without any assertions |
| `SLEEPY_TEST` | Test using `time.Sleep` |
| `REDUNDANT_PRINT` | Test using `fmt.Println` or similar print statements |
| `CONDITIONAL_TEST_LOGIC` | Test containing `if`/`switch` conditional logic |
| `MAGIC_NUMBER_TEST` | Test using magic numbers in assertions |
| `MISSING_ERROR_CHECK` | Test ignoring returned errors |
| `MISSING_HELPER` | Helper function missing `t.Helper()` call |

## Installation

```bash
go install github.com/135yshr/savanna-vet-go/cmd/savanna@latest
```

### Build from source

```bash
git clone https://github.com/135yshr/savanna-vet-go.git
cd savanna-vet-go
go build -o savanna ./cmd/savanna/
```

## Usage

```bash
# Scan the current directory
savanna .

# Scan specific directories
savanna ./pkg ./internal

# Output as JSON
savanna -format json ./...

# Exit with code 1 if smells are found (useful for CI)
savanna -fail ./...

# Enable only specific smells
savanna -enable EMPTY_TEST,SLEEPY_TEST ./...

# Disable specific smells
savanna -disable MAGIC_NUMBER_TEST ./...

# List all available smell types
savanna -list

# Show version
savanna -version

# Output JSON to custom directory
savanna -format json -output ./test-reports ./...
```

### CLI Flags

| Flag | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `-format` | `console` | Report format (`console` or `json`) |
| `-output` | `savanna-reports` | Output directory for JSON reports |
| `-fail` | `false` | Exit with code 1 when smells are detected |
| `-enable` | *(all)* | Comma-separated list of smell types to enable |
| `-disable` | *(none)* | Comma-separated list of smell types to disable |
| `-list` | `false` | Show all available smell types |
| `-version` | `false` | Show version |

### Console Output Example

When test smells are detected, savanna displays a lion ASCII art banner along with the detected smells:

```text
[EMPTY_TEST] example_test.go:10 in TestEmpty() - 空のテストとかお前それ@t_wadaの前でも同じ事言えんの？
[SLEEPY_TEST] example_test.go:20 in TestSlow() - テストでsleepとかお前それ@t_wadaの前でも同じ事言えんの？
```

## License

[MIT](LICENSE)

## Acknowledgments

This project is inspired by [savanna-maven-plugin](https://github.com/kawasima/savanna-maven-plugin) by [@kawasima](https://github.com/kawasima), a test smell detector for Java/Maven projects.

# Smart Commit with Gitmoji

Execute the following steps non-interactively:

## Branch Management

- If currently on `main` or `master` branch, create and checkout a new feature branch with a descriptive name
- If on any other branch, proceed with commit on current branch (no branch creation)
- If no changes are detected, exit without doing anything

## Change Analysis & Commit

1. **Review all changes** using `git status` and `git diff --staged` (or `git diff` if nothing staged)
2. **Stage changes** if not already staged using `git add .` or selective staging
3. **Analyze the changes** to understand:
   - Type of changes (new feature, bug fix, refactor, etc.)
   - Scope of changes (which files/components affected)
   - Impact and purpose of the changes

## Commit Message Creation

Create a commit message following this format:

```
<gitmoji> <type>: <concise description>

<optional detailed explanation if needed>
```

### Gitmoji Selection Guide

Choose the most appropriate gitmoji from <https://gitmoji.dev/> based on change type:

**Common Patterns:**

- ✨ `:sparkles:` - New features
- 🐛 `:bug:` - Bug fixes
- 🎨 `:art:` - Code structure/format improvements
- ⚡️ `:zap:` - Performance improvements
- 📝 `:memo:` - Documentation updates
- 🔧 `:wrench:` - Configuration changes
- ♻️ `:recycle:` - Code refactoring
- 🚨 `:rotating_light:` - Fix linter warnings
- ✅ `:white_check_mark:` - Add/update tests
- 🔒️ `:lock:` - Security fixes
- ⬆️ `:arrow_up:` - Upgrade dependencies
- 🚚 `:truck:` - Move/rename files
- 🗑️ `:wastebasket:` - Remove deprecated code

### Message Guidelines

- **Title**: Keep under 50 characters, use imperative mood
- **Body**: Explain "what" and "why", not "how"
- **Language**: Write in English
- **Tone**: Professional and concise

## Final Steps

- Execute the commit with the generated message
- Display a summary of what was committed
- Suggest next steps (push to remote, create PR, etc.)

file.content:
name: Auto Release

on:
  push:
    branches: [main]

concurrency:
  group: auto-release-main
  cancel-in-progress: false

permissions:
  contents: write

jobs:
  release:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd # v6
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0
          persist-credentials: false

      - name: Record existing tags
        id: before
        run: echo "latest=$(git tag -l 'v*' | sort -V | tail -1)" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@53b83947a5a98c8d113130e565377fae1a50d02f # v6
        with:
          node-version: "22"

      - name: Install semantic-release plugins
        run: npm install -g semantic-release@25.0.3 semantic-release-gitmoji@1.6.9 @semantic-release/changelog@6.0.3 @semantic-release/git@10.0.1

      - name: Run semantic-release
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: npx semantic-release

      - name: Get new tag
        id: tag
        run: |
          LATEST=$(git tag -l 'v*' | sort -V | tail -1)
          BEFORE="${{ steps.before.outputs.latest }}"
          if [ "$LATEST" != "$BEFORE" ]; then
            echo "new_tag=$LATEST" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

      - name: Setup Go
        if: steps.tag.outputs.new_tag != ''
        uses: actions/setup-go@4b73464bb391d4059bd26b0524d20df3927bd417 # v6
        with:
          go-version-file: go.mod

      - name: Run GoReleaser
        if: steps.tag.outputs.new_tag != ''
        uses: goreleaser/goreleaser-action@ec59f474b9834571250b370d4735c50f8e2d1e29 # v7
        with:
          version: "~> v2"
          args: release --clean
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

content.text:
**現状ではドキュメント変更でもリリースが実行されます。**

理由は以下の通りです:

1. **ワークフローのトリガー** — `on: push: branches: [main]` なので、mainへのpushは全て実行される（パスフィルタなし）
2. **semantic-release の `releaseRules`** — `:memo:` (📝) が **patch** に含まれている。今回のコミット `📝 docs: ...` はパッチリリースとして扱われ、バージョンが上がってしまう

対処法は2つあります:

### 方法A: ワークフローにパスフィルタを追加
ドキュメントのみの変更ではワークフロー自体を実行しない。

### 方法B: `:memo:` を releaseRules から除外
semantic-release がドキュメントコミットを「リリース不要」と判断する。

**方法Bの方が適切**だと思います。方法Aだとドキュメントとコード変更が混在するコミットでリリースが漏れる可能性があります。方法Bなら semantic-release が各コミットを個別に判断できます。

修正しますか？

# Smart Commit with Gitmoji

Execute the following steps non-interactively:

## Branch Management

- If currently on `main` or `master` branch, create and checkout a new feature branch with a descriptive name
- If on any other branch, proceed with commit on current branch (no branch creation)
- If no changes are detected, exit without doing anything

## Change Analysis & Commit

1. **Review all changes** using `git status` and `git diff --staged` (or `git diff` if nothing staged)
2. **Stage changes** if not already staged using `git add .` or selective staging
3. **Analyze the changes** to understand:
   - Type of changes (new feature, bug fix, refactor, etc.)
   - Scope of changes (which files/components affected)
   - Impact and purpose of the changes

## Commit Message Creation

Create a commit message following this format:

```
<gitmoji> <type>: <concise description>

<optional detailed explanation if needed>
```

### Gitmoji Selection Guide

Choose the most appropriate gitmoji from <https://gitmoji.dev/> based on change type:

**Common Patterns:**

- ✨ `:sparkles:` - New features
- 🐛 `:bug:` - Bug fixes
- 🎨 `:art:` - Code structure/format improvements
- ⚡️ `:zap:` - Performance improvements
- 📝 `:memo:` - Documentation updates
- 🔧 `:wrench:` - Configuration changes
- ♻️ `:recycle:` - Code refactoring
- 🚨 `:rotating_light:` - Fix linter warnings
- ✅ `:white_check_mark:` - Add/update tests
- 🔒️ `:lock:` - Security fixes
- ⬆️ `:arrow_up:` - Upgrade dependencies
- 🚚 `:truck:` - Move/rename files
- 🗑️ `:wastebasket:` - Remove deprecated code

### Message Guidelines

- **Title**: Keep under 50 characters, use imperative mood
- **Body**: Explain "what" and "why", not "how"
- **Language**: Write in English
- **Tone**: Professional and concise

## Final Steps

- Execute the commit with the generated message
- Display a summary of what was committed
- Suggest next steps (push to remote, create PR, etc.)

`multichecker.Main` doesn’t have separate “standard flags” docs on pkg.go.dev; the flag set is defined in internal packages and wired up by the multichecker/singlechecker drivers.

### Standard/common flags (from `go/analysis/internal/analysisflags`)
Defined/parsed here (used by `{single,multi,unit}checker`): ([go.dev](https://go.dev/src/cmd/vendor/golang.org/x/tools/go/analysis/internal/analysisflags/flags.go))

- `-flags` — print analyzer flags in JSON (used by `go vet` to discover valid flags) ([go.dev](https://go.dev/src/cmd/vendor/golang.org/x/tools/go/analysis/internal/analysisflags/flags.go))  
- `-V` — version flag (added by `addVersionFlag`) ([go.dev](https://go.dev/src/cmd/vendor/golang.org/x/tools/go/analysis/internal/analysisflags/flags.go))  
- `-json` — emit JSON output ([go.dev](https://go.dev/src/cmd/vendor/golang.org/x/tools/go/analysis/internal/analysisflags/flags.go))  
- `-c=N` — show N lines of context around offending line ([go.dev](https://go.dev/src/cmd/vendor/golang.org/x/tools/go/analysis/internal/analysisflags/flags.go))  
- `-fix` — apply all suggested fixes ([go.dev](https://go.dev/src/cmd/vendor/golang.org/x/tools/go/analysis/internal/analysisflags/flags.go))  
- `-diff` — with `-fix`, print unified diff instead of updating files ([go.dev](https://go.dev/src/cmd/vendor/golang.org/x/tools/go/analysis/internal/analysisflags/flags.go))  

Also in **multi** mode, `Parse` adds:
- `-NAME` (one per analyzer) to enable/disable analyzers, and `-NAME.flag` for each analyzer’s own flags. ([go.dev](https://go.dev/src/cmd/vendor/golang.org/x/tools/go/analysis/internal/analysisflags/flags.go))

### Driver/debug/profiling flags (from `go/analysis/internal/checker`)
Registered here (used by the analysis driver behind multichecker/singlechecker): ([go.googlesource.com](https://go.googlesource.com/tools/%2B/refs/tags/gopls/v0.14.2-pre.1/go/analysis/internal/checker/checker.go))

- `-debug=fpstv` (facts/parallelism/sanity/timing/verbose) ([go.googlesource.com](https://go.googlesource.com/tools/%2B/refs/tags/gopls/v0.14.2-pre.1/go/analysis/internal/checker/checker.go))

- `-memprofile FILE` ([go.googlesource.com](https://go.googlesource.com/tools/%2B/refs/tags/gopls/v0.14.2-pre.1/go/analysis/internal/checker/checker.go))  
- `-trace FILE` ([go.googlesource.com](https://go.googlesource.com/tools/%2B/refs/tags/gopls/v0.14.2-pre.1/go/analysis/internal/checker/checker.go))  
- `-test` (analyze test files too) ([go.googlesource.com](https://go.googlesource.com/tools/%2B/refs/tags/gopls/v0.14.2-pre.1/go/analysis/internal/checker/checker.go))

1→# savanna
     2→
     3→[![Go Version](https://img.shields.io/github/go-mod/go-version/135yshr/savanna-vet-go)](https://go.dev/)
     4→[![Go Report Card](https://goreportcard.com/badge/github.com/135yshr/savanna-vet-go)](https://goreportcard.com/report/github.com/135yshr/savanna-vet-go)
     5→[![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-yellow.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT)
     6→
     7→A Go CLI tool that detects test smells in Go test files.
     8→
     9→## Overview
    10→
    11→savanna analyzes Go test files using the `go/analysis` framework and detects common test smells — patterns that indicate potential issues with test quality and maintainability. Integrates with `go vet -vettool` for seamless CI/editor support.
    12→
    13→- **`go vet` integration** — works as a standard `vettool`
    14→- **Fast** — leverages `go/analysis` for static analysis
    15→- **Configurable** — enable/disable individual smell detectors
    16→
    17→## Supported Test Smells
    18→
    19→| Smell Type | Description |
    20→|---|---|
    21→| `EMPTY_TEST` | Test function with no statements |
    22→| `MISSING_ASSERTION` | Test function without any assertions |
    23→| `SLEEPY_TEST` | Test using `time.Sleep` |
    24→| `REDUNDANT_PRINT` | Test using `fmt.Println` or similar print statements |
    25→| `CONDITIONAL_TEST_LOGIC` | Test containing `if`/`switch` conditional logic |
    26→| `MAGIC_NUMBER_TEST` | Test using magic numbers in assertions |
    27→| `MISSING_ERROR_CHECK` | Test ignoring returned errors |
    28→| `MISSING_HELPER` | Helper function missing `t.Helper()` call |
    29→
    30→## Installation
    31→
    32→```bash
    33→go install github.com/135yshr/savanna-vet-go/cmd/savanna@latest
    34→go install github.com/135yshr/savanna-vet-go/cmd/savanna-vet@latest
    35→```
    36→
    37→### Build from source
    38→
    39→```bash
    40→git clone https://github.com/135yshr/savanna-vet-go.git
    41→cd savanna-vet-go
    42→go build -o savanna ./cmd/savanna/
    43→go build -o savanna-vet ./cmd/savanna-vet/
    44→```
    45→
    46→## Usage
    47→
    48→### go vet integration (recommended)
    49→
    50→`savanna-vet` を `go vet -vettool` として使用できます。
    51→
    52→```bash
    53→# go vet 経由で実行
    54→go vet -vettool=$(which savanna-vet) ./...
    55→
    56→# ローカルビルドから実行
    57→go vet -vettool=./savanna-vet ./...
    58→```
    59→
    60→### Standalone (multichecker)
    61→
    62→```bash
    63→# Scan the current directory
    64→savanna .
    65→
    66→# Scan specific directories
    67→savanna ./pkg ./internal
    68→
    69→# Output as JSON
    70→savanna -format json ./...
    71→
    72→# Exit with code 1 if smells are found (useful for CI)
    73→savanna -fail ./...
    74→
    75→# Enable only specific smells
    76→savanna -enable EMPTY_TEST,SLEEPY_TEST ./...
    77→
    78→# Disable specific smells
    79→savanna -disable MAGIC_NUMBER_TEST ./...
    80→
    81→# List all available smell types
    82→savanna -list
    83→
    84→# Show version
    85→savanna -version
    86→
    87→# Output JSON to custom directory
    88→savanna -format json -output ./test-reports ./...
    89→```
    90→
    91→### CLI Flags
    92→
    93→| Flag | Default | Description |
    94→|---|---|---|
    95→| `-format` | `console` | Report format (`console` or `json`) |
    96→| `-output` | `savanna-reports` | Output directory for JSON reports |
    97→| `-fail` | `false` | Exit with code 1 when smells are detected |
    98→| `-enable` | *(all)* | Comma-separated list of smell types to enable |
    99→| `-disable` | *(none)* | Comma-separated list of smell types to disable |
   100→| `-list` | `false` | Show all available smell types |
   101→| `-version` | `false` | Show version |
   102→
   103→### Console Output Example
   104→
   105→When test smells are detected, savanna displays a lion ASCII art banner along with the detected smells:
   106→
   107→```text
   108→[EMPTY_TEST] example_test.go:10 in TestEmpty() - 空のテストとかお前それ@t_wadaの前でも同じ事言えんの？
   109→[SLEEPY_TEST] example_test.go:20 in TestSlow() - テストでsleepとかお前それ@t_wadaの前でも同じ事言えんの？
   110→```
   111→
   112→## License
   113→
   114→[MIT](LICENSE)
   115→
   116→## Acknowledgments
   117→
   118→This project is inspired by [savanna-maven-plugin](https://github.com/kawasima/savanna-maven-plugin) by [@kawasima](https://github.com/kawasima), a test smell detector for Java/Maven projects.

[![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-yellow.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT)

A Go CLI tool that detects test smells in Go test files.

## Overview

savanna analyzes Go test files using the `go/analysis` framework and detects common test smells — patterns that indicate potential issues with test quality and maintainability. Integrates with `go vet -vettool` for seamless CI/editor support.

- **`go vet` integration** — works as a standard `vettool`
- **Fast** — leverages `go/analysis` for static analysis
- **Configurable** — enable/disable individual smell detectors

## Supported Test Smells

| Smell Type | Description |
|---|---|
| `EMPTY_TEST` | Test function with no statements |
| `MISSING_ASSERTION` | Test function without any assertions |
| `SLEEPY_TEST` | Test using `time.Sleep` |
| `REDUNDANT_PRINT` | Test using `fmt.Println` or similar print statements |
| `CONDITIONAL_TEST_LOGIC` | Test containing `if`/`switch` conditional logic |
| `MAGIC_NUMBER_TEST` | Test using magic numbers in assertions |
| `MISSING_ERROR_CHECK` | Test ignoring returned errors |
| `MISSING_HELPER` | Helper function missing `t.Helper()` call |

## Installation

```bash
go install github.com/135yshr/savanna-vet-go/cmd/savanna@latest
go install github.com/135yshr/savanna-vet-go/cmd/savanna-vet@latest
```

### Build from source

```bash
git clone https://github.com/135yshr/savanna-vet-go.git
cd savanna-vet-go
go build -o savanna ./cmd/savanna/
go build -o savanna-vet ./cmd/savanna-vet/
```

## Usage

### go vet integration (recommended)

`savanna-vet` を `go vet -vettool` として使用できます。

```bash
# go vet 経由で実行
go vet -vettool=$(which savanna-vet) ./...

# ローカルビルドから実行
go vet -vettool=./savanna-vet ./...
```

### Standalone (multichecker)

```bash
# Scan the current directory
savanna .

# Scan specific directories
savanna ./pkg ./internal

# Output as JSON
savanna -format json ./...

# Exit with code 1 if smells are found (useful for CI)
savanna -fail ./...

# Enable only specific smells
savanna -enable EMPTY_TEST,SLEEPY_TEST ./...

# Disable specific smells
savanna -disable MAGIC_NUMBER_TEST ./...

# List all available smell types
savanna -list

# Show version
savanna -version

# Output JSON to custom directory
savanna -format json -output ./test-reports ./...
```

### CLI Flags

| Flag | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|
| `-format` | `console` | Report format (`console` or `json`) |
| `-output` | `savanna-reports` | Output directory for JSON reports |
| `-fail` | `false` | Exit with code 1 when smells are detected |
| `-enable` | *(all)* | Comma-separated list of smell types to enable |
| `-disable` | *(none)* | Comma-separated list of smell types to disable |
| `-list` | `false` | Show all available smell types |
| `-version` | `false` | Show version |

### Console Output Example

When test smells are detected, savanna displays a lion ASCII art banner along with the detected smells:

```text
[EMPTY_TEST] example_test.go:10 in TestEmpty() - 空のテストとかお前それ@t_wadaの前でも同じ事言えんの？
[SLEEPY_TEST] example_test.go:20 in TestSlow() - テストでsleepとかお前それ@t_wadaの前でも同じ事言えんの？
```

## License

[MIT](LICENSE)

## Acknowledgments

This project is inspired by [savanna-maven-plugin](https://github.com/kawasima/savanna-maven-plugin) by [@kawasima](https://github.com/kawasima), a test smell detector for Java/Maven projects.

input.new_string:
### go vet integration (recommended)

You can use `savanna-vet` as a `go vet -vettool` plugin.

```bash
# Run via go vet
go vet -vettool=$(which savanna-vet) ./...

# Run from a local build
go vet -vettool=./savanna-vet ./...
```

### Standalone (multichecker)

Both `savanna` and `savanna-vet` are built on `multichecker.Main` / `unitchecker.Main` from `golang.org/x/tools/go/analysis`. The following flags are provided by the analysis framework.

```bash
# Analyze packages
savanna ./...

# Output diagnostics as JSON
savanna -json ./...

# Disable a specific analyzer
savanna -emptytest=false ./...

# Show version
savanna -V
```

### CLI Flags

| Flag | Description |
|---|---|
| `-json` | Output diagnostics in JSON format |
| `-V` | Show version |
| `-c N` | Show N lines of context around each diagnostic |
| `-fix` | Apply suggested fixes |
| `-ANALYZER=false` | Disable a specific analyzer (e.g. `-emptytest=false`) |

# Smart Commit with Gitmoji

Execute the following steps non-interactively:

## Branch Management

- If currently on `main` or `master` branch, create and checkout a new feature branch with a descriptive name
- If on any other branch, proceed with commit on current branch (no branch creation)
- If no changes are detected, exit without doing anything

## Change Analysis & Commit

1. **Review all changes** using `git status` and `git diff --staged` (or `git diff` if nothing staged)
2. **Stage changes** if not already staged using `git add .` or selective staging
3. **Analyze the changes** to understand:
   - Type of changes (new feature, bug fix, refactor, etc.)
   - Scope of changes (which files/components affected)
   - Impact and purpose of the changes

## Commit Message Creation

Create a commit message following this format:

```
<gitmoji> <type>: <concise description>

<optional detailed explanation if needed>
```

### Gitmoji Selection Guide

Choose the most appropriate gitmoji from <https://gitmoji.dev/> based on change type:

**Common Patterns:**

- ✨ `:sparkles:` - New features
- 🐛 `:bug:` - Bug fixes
- 🎨 `:art:` - Code structure/format improvements
- ⚡️ `:zap:` - Performance improvements
- 📝 `:memo:` - Documentation updates
- 🔧 `:wrench:` - Configuration changes
- ♻️ `:recycle:` - Code refactoring
- 🚨 `:rotating_light:` - Fix linter warnings
- ✅ `:white_check_mark:` - Add/update tests
- 🔒️ `:lock:` - Security fixes
- ⬆️ `:arrow_up:` - Upgrade dependencies
- 🚚 `:truck:` - Move/rename files
- 🗑️ `:wastebasket:` - Remove deprecated code

### Message Guidelines

- **Title**: Keep under 50 characters, use imperative mood
- **Body**: Explain "what" and "why", not "how"
- **Language**: Write in English
- **Tone**: Professional and concise

## Final Steps

- Execute the commit with the generated message
- Display a summary of what was committed
- Suggest next steps (push to remote, create PR, etc.)
